prevalence.value: common seasonal illness; 10-20% of infants affected annually
prevalence.source: European pediatric respiratory epidemiology syntheses / WHO Europe
prevalence.trend: seasonal peaks; improved prevention options emerging

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case in severe disease; overall burden moderate
burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in preterm and comorbid infants

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; pulse oximetry; RSV testing used selectively; imaging reserved for atypical cases

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands to >1 million infant episodes/season across EU
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care and oxygen pathways

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to pediatric emergency care and oxygen support
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; oxygen and hydration; avoid routine bronchodilators/antibiotics; ICU support for severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Seasonal surges and hospital bed pressure
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust pediatric care systems and emerging RSV prevention strategies for high-risk infants
